description_long: This cowshed features a large, steeply pitched gable roof covered in reddish-brown tiles, providing ample shelter and protection from the elements. The structure is rectangular with a prominent overhanging roof section at the front, likely serving as a sheltered entry or loading area. The walls are solid and appear to be constructed from a combination of wood and concrete, with the lower portion in a light gray tone and the upper section in black. There are no visible windows, and the main entrance is located beneath the extended roof, supported by simple posts. The overall design is utilitarian, prioritizing function and durability, with minimal decorative elements. The building is typical of rural or agricultural settings but could be found on the outskirts of urban environments where livestock facilities are present.
description: A large, rectangular cowshed with a steep red-tiled gable roof, solid gray and black walls, and a sheltered front entry area. The design is functional and robust, with no visible windows and minimal ornamentation.
description_view_0: The front view shows the wide, overhanging red-tiled roof and the sheltered entry area supported by posts.
description_view_1: The side view highlights the steep pitch of the gable roof and the solid, windowless wall.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the full expanse of the roof with no visible openings or features on the back wall.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors the first, showing the gable end and the extension of the roof over the entry area.
attribute_door_style: single wooden plank door under roof overhang
